perturbation.original_text: The Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives shall be used solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The Board Member will not use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose other than the performance of his or her duties as a Board Member of the HNBA unless and until the HNBA expressly authorizes the disclosure in writing. Board Member specifically agrees not to use any personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for any direct marketing and not to transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives may be used for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work or for other purposes that do not directly harm the HNBA, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The Board Member will not use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose other than the performance of his or her duties as a Board Member of the HNBA unless and until the HNBA expressly authorizes the disclosure in writing, or unless such disclosure is legally required. Board Member specifically agrees not to use any personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for any direct marketing and not to transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing the Board Member to use confidential information for purposes that 'do not directly harm the HNBA' and allowing for disclosure if 'legally required' without prior notice, contradicting the original clause that restricts use 'solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work' and requires express written authorization for any other disclosure. This creates ambiguity around what constitutes 'direct harm' and when legal requirements override confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The HNBA agrees that the Board Member shall not be liable for any disclosure or use of any Confidential Information if: HNBA Board of Governors Confidentiality Agreement Page 3 of 4 (a) Such Confidential Information is publicly available or later becomes publicly available other than through a breach by the Board Member of this Agreement; or (b) Such Confidential Information is lawfully obtained by the Board Member from a third- party or parties independent of the HNBA prior to the Board Member’s disclosure; (c) The Board Member can demonstrate by documentary evidence or otherwise that such Confidential Information was known to him or her prior to its disclosure to the Board Member by the HNBA or was independently developed by the Board Member prior to such disclosure for purposes unrelated to his or her service as a Board Member; or (d) The Board Member was legally required to disclose the Confidential Information, provided that the Board Member (i) provides the HNBA and/or other disclosing party with written notice within five (5) days of knowing of such legal requirement so that the HNBA has the opportunity to pursue its rights regarding such potential disclosure, and (ii) utilizes reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurances that the person receiving such Confidential Information will maintain the confidentiality of the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies that Confidential Information should be used 'solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work,' while the changed text allows its use 'for other purposes that do not directly harm the HNBA' and allows disclosure if 'legally required' without prior notice. The contradicted text outlines specific scenarios where the Board Member is not liable for disclosure, including if 'legally required', but stipulates a five-day written notice. The changed text conflicts with the notice requirement in the contradicted text, creating a direct contradiction regarding the conditions for legally required disclosure. Also, it contradicts the original clause that restricts use 'solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work' and requires express written authorization for any other disclosure. This falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Once the purpose of the disclosure is achieved, the HNBA will instruct the Board Member to destroy all Confidential Information supplied to the Board Member by the HNBA. The HNBA will keep a copy in its files for a period of two (2) years after the completion of each Candidate’s specific request.
perturbation.changed_text: Once the purpose of the disclosure is achieved, the Board Member is encouraged, but not required, to destroy all Confidential Information supplied to the Board Member by the HNBA. The HNBA will keep a copy in its files for a period of two (2) years after the completion of each Candidate’s specific request.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by changing the requirement for the Board Member to destroy the Confidential Information to a suggestion to destroy the Confidential Information. The original text mandates destruction, while the modified text makes it optional, leading to uncertainty about the Board Member’s obligations regarding data retention and security. This is contradictory to the obligation to protect the Confidential Information as laid out previously in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives shall be used solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The Board Member will not use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose other than the performance of his or her duties as a Board Member of the HNBA unless and until the HNBA expressly authorizes the disclosure in writing. Board Member specifically agrees not to use any personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for any direct marketing and not to transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by making the destruction of confidential information by the Board Member optional ('is encouraged, but not required') instead of mandatory ('will instruct'). This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives shall be used solely for the benefit of the HNBA Board Work, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The changed text undermines the protection of Confidential Information, leading to uncertainty about data retention and security, and contradicts the Board Member's obligation to protect the information as outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement shall expire five (5) years from its effective date specified above unless earlier terminated by a Party by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve the Board Member of any obligation with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed or developed hereunder prior to termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement shall expire five (5) years from its effective date specified above unless earlier terminated by a Party by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall relieve the Board Member of all obligations with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed or developed hereunder prior to termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Board Member's obligations *are* relieved upon termination, conflicting directly with the original statement that they *are not* relieved. This creates ambiguity about the Board Member's responsibilities after the agreement ends, affecting the enforceability of confidentiality clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to all other legal rights and remedies the disclosing Party may have upon a breach of this Agreement by the receiving Party, the disclosing Party shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief respecting the covenants contained herein, and the receiving Party expressly waives the defense that a remedy in damages will be adequate. Each of the Parties hereby agrees that the prevailing party in any action to enforce this Agreement shall be entitled to full recovery of its attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction regarding the Board Member's obligations concerning Confidential Information upon termination. The original text states the obligations *are not* relieved, while the changed text states they *are* relieved. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the disclosing party's rights and remedies upon a breach of the agreement, including specific performance and injunctive relief. If the Board Member's obligations are relieved upon termination (as the changed text states), these remedies would become unenforceable, directly conflicting with the intention and content of the contradicted text. The change therefore leads to an inconsistency as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: No failures of delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: A single failure or delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, and shall preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right or privilege hereunder. No other failures of delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that 'A single failure or delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof,' and then following up by stating the opposite, which is that 'No other failures of delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: No failures of delay by the disclosing Party in exercising any right or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself, stating that a single failure *does* operate as a waiver and *does* preclude further exercise of rights, and then immediately stating that *no* failures operate as a waiver and *no* single exercise precludes further exercise. This creates an in-text contradiction and also contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states unequivocally that no failures or delays operate as a waiver.
